chapters.caption: Utterson stands before the blistered, blank-faced door set into the wall of Jekyll's laboratory in a London side street.
chapters.attribution: Charles Raymond Macauley, 1904, Scott-Thaw edition. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Hyde beats Sir Danvers Carew to death with a weighted cane in the street.
chapters.attribution: Charles Raymond Macauley, 1904, Scott-Thaw edition. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Utterson and Poole stand together in the darkness outside Jekyll's locked laboratory on the last night.
chapters.attribution: Charles Raymond Macauley, 1904, Scott-Thaw edition. Public domain.

chapters.caption: Mr Hyde, the irreversibly dominant alter ego, confronts the reader in Jekyll's full written confession.
chapters.attribution: Charles Raymond Macauley, 1904, Scott-Thaw edition. Public domain.
